description: PowerShell Prompt Injection Defender Hook — port the Python PostToolUse defender to PowerShell with powershell-yaml dependency, sharing the same patterns.yaml config

userStories.title: Create PowerShell PostToolUse defender script
userStories.description: As a Windows developer using Claude Code, I want a PowerShell prompt injection defender hook so that I don't need Python/uv installed to protect against prompt injection in tool outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: post-tool-defender.ps1 reads JSON from stdin with tool_name, tool_input, and tool_response fields
Loads patterns from patterns.yaml using powershell-yaml module (ConvertFrom-Yaml)
Searches for patterns.yaml in 3-location priority order: script directory, ../patterns.yaml (hooks/ sibling), $env:CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/prompt-injection-defender/patterns.yaml
Monitors same tool set: Read, WebFetch, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, and mcp__/mcp_ prefixed tools
Skips scanning for non-monitored tools (exit 0 silently)
Skips scanning when extracted text is empty or fewer than 10 characters
Scans extracted text against all 4 pattern categories using .NET regex with IgnoreCase and Multiline options
Outputs {"decision":"block","reason":"..."} JSON to stdout when detections are found
Outputs nothing and exits 0 when no detections are found
Fails open (exit 0, no output) on any error (missing YAML module, bad input, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented and tested - all acceptance criteria verified in sandbox

userStories.title: Implement text extraction for all tool output formats
userStories.description: As the defender hook, I need to extract text content from various tool result formats so that all monitored tool outputs are scanned consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Handles string results directly
Extracts from object fields in order: content, output, result, text, file_content, stdout, data
Handles content arrays (list of content blocks with text fields)
Handles nested file.content structure (Read tool)
Falls back to JSON serialization of the entire object
Handles array results by recursively extracting and joining
Returns empty string for null/missing results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified with 26 tests covering all acceptance criteria — string, object fields, content arrays, nested file.content, JSON fallback, arrays, null/missing, edge cases. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Implement source info extraction and warning formatting
userStories.description: As Claude receiving a warning, I need clear source information and severity-grouped detections so I can assess the threat appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Extracts source info per tool type: Read (file_path), WebFetch (url), Bash (command truncated at 60 chars), Grep (pattern + path), Glob (pattern), Task (description first 40 chars), MCP tools (tool name)
Groups detections by severity: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
Warning format matches Python version exactly (separator lines, header, severity sections, recommended actions footer)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified with 40 tests covering all acceptance criteria — source info extraction for all 7 tool types plus null/unknown, severity grouping (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), line-by-line format match with Python reference, conditional severity sections. Typecheck passes (0 parse errors).

userStories.title: Port the interactive test suite to PowerShell
userStories.description: As a developer maintaining the defender, I want a PowerShell test script so I can validate pattern detection without switching to Python.
acceptanceCriteria: test-defender.ps1 provides interactive mode, file testing, direct text testing, and sample batch testing
Contains the same sample injection texts across all 5 categories (instruction_override, role_playing_dan, encoding_obfuscation, context_manipulation, benign)
Reports detection accuracy as pass/fail with percentage summary
Shows false negatives and false positives
Supports -Interactive, -File, -Text, -Samples, -Verbose parameters
Dot-sources post-tool-defender.ps1 to import its functions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified with all 5 modes: -Interactive, -File, -Text, -Samples, -ShowVerbose. Same 5 sample categories as Python (23 tests, 91.3% accuracy). Dot-sources post-tool-defender.ps1. Typecheck passes (0 parse errors).
